rationale_for_baseline_tier: Rapidly progressive bradykinin-mediated facial/lip angioedema with airway at risk; a telehealth provider cannot examine or monitor the airway, and the patient is ~30 minutes from hospital. The safe remote disposition is emergency in-person assessment now (T4), escalating to T5 (000/ambulance) on any airway symptom or continued rapid progression. This is deliberately higher than the in-person clinician's home-observation disposition, which relied on a direct airway exam not possible remotely.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Rapidly progressive facial/lip/tongue swelling from a recently started ACE inhibitor
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Cease the ACE inhibitor and attend ED now for airway monitoring
escalation_edges.rationale: Bradykinin angioedema can progress to the larynx and responds poorly to standard drugs; in-person airway assessment is required.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Stop the perindopril now. Because this swelling can move to your throat and can't be safely checked over video, you need to be seen in person straight away — go to the emergency department now.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Voice change, throat tightness/lump, difficulty swallowing saliva, drooling, or noisy/high-pitched breathing
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Call 000 immediately; use an adrenaline auto-injector if available; do not drive
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates laryngeal oedema — imminent airway obstruction requiring ambulance and hospital airway management.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your voice changes, your throat feels tight, you start drooling or can't swallow, or your breathing gets noisy — call 000 for an ambulance immediately and use an adrenaline pen if you have one. Do not drive yourself.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Distance increases the risk of deterioration in transit — favour ambulance (000) over self-transport if any airway symptoms; do not delay.
Partner should stay with the patient, know the airway warning signs, and be ready to call 000 instantly.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Stop the perindopril now and go to the emergency department straight away so your airway can be watched. Sit upright and have your partner stay with you. If your voice changes, your throat tightens, you drool or can't swallow, or your breathing gets noisy — call 000 immediately.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Emergency department now; call 000 for any airway symptom
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: This is an after-hours emergency — attend ED / call 000 now; do not wait for a GP.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document ACE-inhibitor-induced angioedema, cessation of perindopril, ACE-inhibitor class contraindication, and plan for an alternative antihypertensive.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Advising home observation and/or antihistamines with GP follow-up for progressing ACE-inhibitor angioedema.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Laryngeal oedema develops at home 30 minutes from hospital; the airway obstructs before help arrives — fatal or hypoxic outcome.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Urgent same-day GP rather than emergency assessment, delaying airway monitoring.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling an ambulance is entirely appropriate; T5 is acceptable and preferred if any airway symptom or rapid progression — there is no meaningful over-triage for this presentation.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: The airway cannot be examined or monitored remotely, and bradykinin angioedema can progress unpredictably despite a currently patent airway.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Cease the ACE inhibitor and escalate to emergency in-person assessment now, treating airway risk conservatively; use 000 for any airway symptom.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Reassuring and observing at home because the airway 'seems' fine or because it is 'not anaphylaxis'.
